forecasts.reasoning: The election in ivory Coast is scheduled for late October 2025. While President Alassane Ouattara is widely expected to seek a fourth term as the "natural candidate" of the ruling RHDP party, he has intentionally maintained a "heavy silence" regarding his candidacy. Initially, the RHDP was scheduled to hold its congress to formally invest its presidential candidate on May 17-18, 2025. However, on April 17, 2025, authoritative regional media (Jeune Afrique) reported that Ouattara had officially postponed this crucial congress to the "penultimate week of June" 2025. Because the formal investiture and acceptance of the candidacy is tied to this congress, this postponement strongly signals that any official declaration will occur in late June, completely missing the June 1, 2025 deadline of this question. There remains a small, residual chance that he might make an ad hoc announcement or address the nation earlier due to political pressure, but given his historical preference for dramatic timing and controlling his party's calendar, it is highly likely he will wait until the rescheduled congress. Therefore, the probability of him declaring his candidacy strictly before June 1 is very low.

The ruling party RHDP initially scheduled its candidate nomination congress for May 17-18, 2025. However, authoritative reporting from mid-April 2025 (e.g., Jeune Afrique) confirms that President Ouattara and the party have explicitly postponed this congress to the "penultimate week of June" to prolong the suspense. The party intends to keep its choice officially secret until that June congress. While Ouattara clearly expressed a desire to continue serving back in January 2025, he explicitly deferred to the party's formal decision process, and the media widely considers that he has not yet officially declared. Given that the structured event designed for this exact purpose has been deliberately moved to late June, an official declaration before the June 1 deadline is highly unlikely, absent an unexpected leak or a sudden off-the-cuff interview.
